mnemonics.title: Spiral Model Phases
mnemonics.content: P-R-E-P: Planning → Risk Analysis → Engineering → Evaluation (and this Repeats in a cycle)

mnemonics.title: Evolutionary Models Key Features
mnemonics.content: C-H-A-N-G-E: Continuous feedback → Handling changing requirements → Adaptive planning → Iterative development → Non-linear progression → Goal-oriented → Evolving software

acronyms.usage: An evolutionary model emphasizing fast development through iterative components and reusable modules
An iterative software development framework that is inherently evolutionary, using use-case driven and architecture-centric approaches

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a spiral starting from the center (project initiation) and expanding outward (each loop represents an iteration with increasing completeness). The width of the spiral at each cycle represents the project's scope and risk level at that phase.
Draw a straight line for sequential models (Waterfall) and a curving, branching tree for evolutionary models showing iterations and evolving requirements.
Think of a sketch before a painting - a quick, imperfect representation that helps refine the final masterpiece (the actual software system).

analogies.analogy: Like a roller coaster that spirals up - each loop (iteration) increases your height (project completion) while you assess and manage thrills (risks) at each level.
Like raising a child - they don't become adults instantly but evolve through stages (iterations) with continuous learning and adaptation.
Like test-driving a car before buying - you get a feel for what you want, identify issues, and then make an informed decision about the final product.
